title: Drip vs Campaign Monitor (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Drip and Campaign Monitor. See pricing, features, and which email marketing tool suits your business best.
h1: Drip vs Campaign Monitor: Complete Comparison
intro: Drip and Campaign Monitor are both established email marketing platforms, but they cater to different business needs. Drip specializes in e-commerce automation with revenue tracking, while Campaign Monitor excels at multi-brand management for agencies. Understanding their strengths will help you choose the right tool for your marketing strategy.

comparison_table.tool_a: E-commerce businesses with deep store integration needs
comparison_table.tool_b: Agencies and teams managing multiple brands

comparison_table.tool_a: E-commerce tracking, revenue attribution, visual automation, SMS
comparison_table.tool_b: Template builder, journey designer, multi-brand management, transactional email

choose_a_if: You run an e-commerce store and need advanced revenue tracking and attribution
SMS marketing capabilities are essential to your email marketing strategy
You want a visual automation builder specifically designed for online retailers
choose_b_if: Budget is a primary concern and you want to start at $11/month
You manage multiple brands or client accounts requiring multi-brand management
You need beautiful email templates and a journey designer for campaign creation

faq.q: Is Drip better than Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: Neither is universally better—it depends on your needs. Drip excels for e-commerce businesses needing revenue attribution and SMS, earning a higher G2 rating of 4.4. Campaign Monitor is better for agencies managing multiple brands with a lower starting price of $11/month. Choose based on your specific use case.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Drip or Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: Campaign Monitor is significantly cheaper, starting at $11/month compared to Drip's $39/month. However, Drip's additional e-commerce features and SMS capabilities may justify the higher cost for online retailers. Consider your feature requirements when evaluating total cost of ownership.

faq.q: Can I switch from Campaign Monitor to Drip?
faq.a: Yes, most email marketing platforms support importing your subscriber lists and campaign data. Both Drip and Campaign Monitor allow data exports and migrations, though you'll need to manually recreate automations and templates. Plan your migration during a low-activity period to minimize disruption.
